Set in the near future, the stories revolve around the evil corporation, 4Ever Innovations, whose young and brilliant scientists download and sell memories. They replace those we've lost with clones and devise technology that helps us live out our greatest fantasies; from saving a failed relationship, to creating the perfect lover, to being irresistible to the opposite sex - but sometimes our deepest desires come at a terrible price. 4Ever offers customers the opportunity to live out their fantasies in a virtual world but there are secrets within 4Ever Innovations that may cost you your life.

programme.desc: Blue World travels to the Maldives to witness a huge Manta ray feeding frenzy and then visits Indonesia to explore an island surrounded by venomous sea snakes!

programme.title: Hot Water Ep105: V8 Superboat Championships from Griffith in New South Wales, Australia
programme.desc: Hot Water showcases the world’s best in adrenalized water-related motorsports. This episode features Round 5 of Jet Sprinting from Cabarita in New South Wales, Australia. V8 Unlimited Superboats are the top racing class in Jet Sprinting with the boats being unrestricted.

programme.title: Farm Hero
programme.desc: This week, host Laura visits a family who has taken backyard farming to a whole new level. The Killpatrick family led by husband and father Michael is paving the way for the next generation of regenerative farming. This farm sprouted from the 2020 pandemic and in a few short years has become a pivotal cornerstone of their city community for all things holistic and organic. The Farm on Central is proving what can be accomplished with dedication to the future of farming.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Surviving Mann returns with its most punishing season opener yet. In true Don Mann fashion, the bar is raised with a grueling 10-part physical readiness test—infused with a blast of hypothermia for good measure. Don himself calls it “the most brutal PT [he’s] ever witnessed,” and it’s clear the contestants are in for a world of hurt. To push them even further, MMA legend Ken Shamrock joins the fray, bringing his no-nonsense intensity to whip the group into fighting shape. Those who survive the trial by ice and exertion are rewarded with a chance to test-fire the lightest AR on the market—if they can still feel their trigger fingers.

programme.title: Boom America
programme.desc: This week is all about health and wellness. Kyle Reyes is back with Nuethix for an inspection into the inner workings of their company.. and his health. Then, the team gets a closer look at Dr. Price’s chiropractor practice and his unique start to the vitamin operations game. Then, get your questions about Clear Water quenched as we take a deeper dive into the company and their global legacy. To cap off the episode, two A-Team members head over to check out Stream2Sea’s innovative factory.

programme.title: Boom America
programme.desc: Today, Kevin and Toli sit down with a new batch of business owners looking to grow and thrive. This first business owner is the heart of America personified. Mark Baylis founded Valor for struggling veterans around America and now it’s time for the A-team to help him scale his mission to reach more patriots in need. From there, the team touch base with a rough and tumble businessman who brings bespoke adventure experiences to life. After that, the pioneers of STAYCA VACA bring their niche business model to the team for evaluation. Then, Compliant Tech caps off the day with a shock to the system.

programme.title: Beyond the Close
programme.desc: Our realtors were already superstars, but now it’s time to move them even higher. In this episode, you’ll learn what it takes to win at the highest levels from the 30-billion-dollar-man Jay Abraham, SEO wizard AJ Aluthwala, leadership powerhouse Nate Bailey, and mindset genius Michael Bernoff. They’ll each share game-changing strategies to take our superstars (any anyone watching) into the stratosphere.

programme.title: S3E1 Animals
programme.desc: GoPro Animals delivers heartwarming, hilarious, and jaw-dropping moments from the animal world. From Golden Retrievers, French Bulldogs, and Border Collies bringing endless puppy smiles, to rescuing orcas and swimming with humpback whales, this show has it all. Wildlife photographer Chris Barnard captures the wild up close, even surviving a giraffe’s mighty kick. Plus, join Ocean Conservation Namibia as they race to save seals trapped in deadly ocean debris. A must-watch for every animal lover.

programme.title: S1E1 Lucha
programme.desc: Ian Hodgkinson, known as Vampiro in the world of wrestling, was a famed wrestler in the late 80’s and 90’s. Today, he shifted his career to being behind the scenes as a Story Producer for AAA, the top wrestling company in Lucha Libre. Hodgkinson intimately peels back the curtain on the mysteries of this show, how it’s produced, and what life is like on and off the stage. We dive deep in to the lives of two wrestlers and follow their stories as they prep for their next fight: El Hijo Del Fantasma, an up and coming star who seemingly has it all, as well as Faby Apache, a veteran female wrestler struggling to stay relevant as she raises her son.

programme.title: Inside Pro Surfing: Rip Curl Pro Bells Beach presented by Bonsoy 2025
programme.desc: Bells Beach on Victoria's South Coast is the longest-running surfing competition and will host the 62st edition this year. Bells is Australian surfing heritage. With the hope of scoring long-period, groomed walls, the surfers will need to display the perfect combination of power, precision, and timing. The biggest surfing legends have had the honor of winning and ringing ‘The Bell' and 2025 will see a hungry new generation vying for the opportunity to cement their names in one of surfing's most prestigious events.

programme.title: African Renaissance | Brilliant Corners: Senegal, Episode 3
programme.desc: Sam, Easkey, and Adrien catch up with West African surf legend Oumar Seye, the first West African sponsored surfer. Seye has impacted the surf culture in Senegal in more ways than one, both proving the sport can be more than a hobby and supporting sustainability by banning plastic bags at his surf events. They end their trip in Popenguine, a bohemian beach town where an incredible women-led initiative to plant thousands of trees has made the area one of the greenest parts of the country.
